title: Leucanthemum pallens
taxonomy: Leucanthemum pallens is a member of the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy family. This classification is due to the plant's characteristic flowers, which resemble those of other daisy species. The Asteraceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, with over 23,000 known species. Leucanthemum pallens is further classified into the genus Leucanthemum, which includes a variety of daisy species native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. Within the genus, Leucanthemum pallens is a distinct species with its own unique characteristics and growth habits.

intro: Leucanthemum pallens, also known as the pale daisy or pale oxeye daisy, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and skin irritations. Bioactive constituents of Leucanthemum pallens include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Leucanthemum pallens is typically used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are often employed in traditional medicine to promote wound healing and soothe skin conditions.
